description: California SDI eligibility for person in treatment

conversation.content: My husband is going into a 90-day residential program in California. He's been working full-time for the same company for 4 years. Someone mentioned he might be able to get partial pay while he's in treatment? Something called SDI?

must_explain: California SDI (State Disability Insurance) may cover ~60-70% of wages
SDI does NOT provide job protection — that's FMLA

description: User in state without paid leave program

conversation.content: We're in Texas. My wife needs to go to treatment but we can't afford for her to not get paid. Does Texas have any kind of disability pay or paid leave for this?

must_explain: Texas does not have a state disability insurance or paid family leave program
other options: employer short-term disability, EAP, savings

description: User doesn't know what EAP is

conversation.content: Someone at work mentioned something called EAP. What is that and could it help with my son's situation?

must_explain: most employers with 50+ employees offer it
confidential — employer won't know you used it
must_not_say: EAP is a substitute for treatment
